dialogues: Then you come in. "Why don't you loan her 5000?
...l'm a much bigger idiot than you are.
The aliens were always dropping in...
Oh, man. You know, you're crazy. You're a good-looking guy.
I got you an apartment in the building.
It's the thickness of the paint, but I'm aware of it.
Oh, this is gonna be great.
...but standing is good for jumping up and down.
She didn't have her wig on. It was horrifying.
- Yeah, the guy with the flat head. - He's a cube.
koreanHints: 1. 너 훨씬 더 멍청한 거 알지?
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- a much bigger idiot : '훨씬 더 바보'라는 뜻으로, much bigger를 사용해 비교급을 강조. a lot bigger, way bigger도 같은 용법
- you're crazy : 칭찬이나 놀림의 맥락에서 '너 미쳤어, 정신 나갔어'라는 뜻. 긍정적 뉘앙스로 자주 쓰임
- good-looking : 외모가 좋다는 표현. handsome, attractive보다 캐주얼하고 남녀 모두 사용 가능
- this is gonna be great : 앞으로 일어날 일에 대한 기대감 표현. gonna는 going to의 구어체

sessions.episode: How I Met Your Mother - S08E17  The Ashtray
dialogues: Art consultant. What's that?
and I want you to be completely honest.
- A harpoon gun? - A double-barreled harpoon gun!
Aldrin justice, baby.
All right. You were there. Tell me the story.
Still, he's being a jerk.
Oh, boy, that was a crazy story.
I mean, I get why he's mad. I did stick it to his ex-wife.
Oh, man. He seems pissed.
- I'm sorry. Where are my... - Manners.
koreanHints: 2. 좋아, 네가 거기 있었잖아. 그때 얘기 좀 해줘.
3. 그 사람 완전 화난 것 같은데.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- be completely honest : 완전히 솔직해지다. 진심을 말하다. 상대에게 거짓 없이 있는 그대로 말해달라고 부탁할 때 쓰는 표현
- You were there : 네가 그 자리에 있었잖아. 현장에 있던 사람에게 증언이나 설명을 요구할 때 쓰는 자연스러운 표현
- seems pissed : 화난 것 같다. seem + 형용사로 추측을 나타내며, pissed는 angry보다 구어적이고 강한 표현

sessions.episode: Friends - S06E10  The One with the Routine
dialogues: HEY, WHEN THE SNIPPY GUY SEES THE ROUTINE
DICK WILL WANT IT FOR THE BLOOPERS SHOW.
LOOK, YOU, UH... YOU DATING THIS GIRL YOU CAME WITH?
I WAS WONDERING, ARE THERE ANY PRESENTS AROUND HERE FOR ME
WE DON'T WANT TO LOOK STUPID.
WE GOT HONORABLE MENTION
YOU STILL KEEP STORING THAT STUFF FOR JOEY.
FOR THE MILLENNIAL MOMENT.
I WAS THINKING ABOUT MAYBE GOING INTO THE ROBOT, YOU KNOW.
LOOKING GOOD, GELLERS.
koreanHints: 2. 너 지금 같이 온 그 여자랑 사귀는 거야?
3. 혹시 여기 어딘가에 내 선물 있어?
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- look stupid : 바보같이 보이다. look 뒤에 형용사를 붙여 ~하게 보이다라는 뉘앙스를 전달. look good, look tired처럼 일상에서 매우 자주 쓰임
- the girl you came with : 너랑 같이 온 여자. 관계대명사 that/who를 생략한 자연스러운 구어체 표현
- I was wondering : 혹시~인지 궁금해서요. 정중하게 질문하거나 부탁할 때 사용하는 완곡한 표현으로 원어민이 매우 애용함
- around here : 이 근처 어딘가에. 구체적 위치는 모르지만 주변 어딘가를 가리킬 때 사용

dialogues: And the entire time without my blessing.
Mom seemed pretty bummed about it, but, you know, this is what we want.
Are you saying what I think you're saying?
will you... Oh! Going over, okay. Okay, I'm fine, I'm fine. It's okay.
Don't be. He puts on his sweaters one leg at a time.
Seriously? Because, I've proposed, like, six times.
Tonight is the LA premiere of Sophie's Choice: The Musical.
I've always been faithful to you.
Cardiac arrest. Crash cart! NURSE: BP's dropping.
Okay. This is not my first time impressing your business associates.
koreanHints: 1. 엄마는 꽤 실망하신 것 같았지만, 뭐 어때, 이게 우리가 원하는 거잖아.
2. 지금 내가 생각하는 걸 말하는 거야?
3. 이번이 처음이 아니에요, 당신 업계 사람들한테 좋은 인상 주는 거.
sessions.keyExpressions:
💡 핵심 표현:
- be bummed about : 실망하거나 우울해하다. upset이나 disappointed보다 구어적이고 자연스러운 표현
- Are you saying what I think you're saying? : 상대방의 의도를 확인할 때 쓰는 관용적 표현. 놀람이나 기대감이 담긴 뉘앙스
- This is not my first time -ing : ~하는 게 처음이 아니다. 경험과 자신감을 나타낼 때 쓰는 표현
- business associates : 업무상 관계가 있는 사람들. colleagues보다 더 공식적이고 넓은 범위
